title: How to build with WebSockets support
body:
I'm attempting to build v. 1.6.4 with WebSockets support and it's not working.
I get no compile-time error. However, when I run the broker, the log file reports that websockets is not supported, "Error: Websockets support not available."

I've compiled using `WITH_WEBSOCKETS=yes`, and I have `libwebsockets` installed, but still no success. A look at `config.mk` suggests that there's no way to tell the build chain where to get libwebsockets from.

To include WebSocket support, the instructions say to `make WITH_WEBSOCKETS=yes` but that seems insufficient in and of itself. What am I doing wrong?
resolution:
Mosquitto has two ways of building. The first, and best supported, is the use of the plain makefiles provided. The second is to use cmake, for Windows and Mac, and for people who don't want to use the plain makefiles for whatever reason.

If you're using the plain makefiles, then `make WITH_WEBSOCKETS=yes` should be sufficient for most people. Specifying include/library paths can be done using `make WITH_WEBSOCKETS=yes CFLAGS="-I<path to include>"  LDFLAGS="-L<path to lib>"`.

If you're using then try `cmake -DWITH_WEBSOCKETS=yes -DCMAKE_C_FLAGS="-I<path to include>" -DCMAKE_EXE_LINKER_FLAGS="-L<path to lib>"`

I hope that helps.

title: Broker does not disconnect clients exceeding the server maximum packet size
body:
The packet size boundary at which the server disconnects clients starts a few bytes later than what the server actually has configured.

Test setup:
- connect a client to a default configured broker
- the broker returns Server Maximum Packet Size = 2_000_000
- client publishes to topic "a" with QoS 0 a message with length 1_999_993
- this packet has a total size of 2_000_001 = 4 (fixed header) + 3 (topic) + 1 (property length) + 1_999_993 (message))

The broker keeps the connection alive and does not send a DISCONNECT packet or close the network connection.

The disconnect only happens when the packet reaches a total size of 2_000_004, presumably because the fixed header encoding is not included in the packet size calculation.

> The Client MUST NOT send packets exceeding Maximum Packet Size to the Server [MQTT-3.2.2-15]. If a Server receives a packet whose size exceeds this limit, this is a Protocol Error, the Server uses DISCONNECT with Reason Code 0x95 (Packet too large), as described in [section 4.13](https://docs.oasis-open.org/mqtt/mqtt/v5.0/os/mqtt-v5.0-os.html#S4_13_Errors).

While this is not a normative requirement, as far as I am concerned this is still a bug because receiving such a packet is a protocol error and the server must close the connection:

> When a Server detects a Malformed Packet or Protocol Error, and a Reason Code is given in the specification, it MUST close the Network Connection [MQTT-4.13.1-1]

title: Feature request: reduce unnecessary network traffic
body:
I am referring to a case where N brokers build a network mirroring each others topics. 

1. It seems to me that a variant of QOS0 on topics with retained data should have an option
"publish at most once, but not if payload data is identical to (already stored) retained data." Maybe implemented with an additional flag. 

This would cause a temperature sensor reading the same temperature every second not causing all brokers to repeatly broadcast this again and again. Of course there might be cases where the time and the occurance itself of the identical data is important, but in other cases it is not.

2. To bridge, an additional mode should be configured where broker A can mirror a topic of broker B, but only actually subscribes to the topic on broker B, if at least one client is subscribed for it at broker A. And this should be handled dynamically. 

Currently broker A can subscribe to topics on broker B or push its own topics to it. In this case data is transmitted to broker B even if nobody listens there.
resolution:
Hm, I cannot follow: How would retained messages be missed? When the mirroring broker resubscribes to the topic, it will get the retained message as a first one and can pass this to his subscribers (which also just that moment have subscribed).

The flow would be: 
0. Broker B has no subscribers to the topic and therefore unsubscribes from the topic on broker A, thus throw away any retained messge (or flag them  as old).
1. a client subscribes on broker B to the topic. There is no retained message at that moment. 
3. Immedeately broker B (re)subscribes at broker A to the topic and eventually receives a reteined message.
4. broker B forwards that retained message  to the cient (with retained flag set).

I do not see the problem.

Its never as simple. :-)

title: How to fix  Error: Duplicate password_file value in configuration.
body:
Hello, I have been configuring the broker with the following parameters, but when checking if there is any problem with the configuration with the command `$ sudo mosquitto -v -c /etc/mosquitto/mosquitto.conf`   it throws me an error.
this:
1598237117: Loading config file /etc/mosquitto/conf.d/default.conf
1598237117: Error: Duplicate password_file value in configuration.
1598237117: Error found at /etc/mosquitto/mosquitto.conf:29.


The broker configuration is as follows:


```
# Place your local configuration in /etc/mosquitto/conf.d/
#
# A full description of the configuration file is at
# /usr/share/doc/mosquitto/examples/mosquitto.conf.example

pid_file /var/run/mosquitto.pid

persistence true
persistence_location / var / lib / mosquitto /

log_dest file /var/log/mosquitto/mosquitto.log

include_dir /etc/mosquitto/conf.d

port 1883

log_type error
log_type notice
log_type information
log_type debug
log_dest topic
log_type warning

allow_anonymous false
connection_messages true
max_keepalive 10
log_timestamp true

password_file / etc / mosquitto / passwd

################################################## ##
# MOSQUITTO LISTENER WITH SLL
################################################## ##

listener 8883

cafile /etc/mosquitto/certs/ca.crt
certfile /etc/mosquitto/certs/server.crt
keyfile /etc/mosquitto/certs/server.key

require_certificate true
use_identity_as_username true
tls_version tlsv1.2
```

Does anyone know how I can solve this?
resolution: include_dir includes the configurations from the given file to the main file. Therefore you have password_file settings twice. Once in your main file and once in /etc/mosquitto/conf.d.  You end up using the ports 1883 & 8883 twice in your configurations aswell.

title: Current release problems with MacOS Catalina
body:
Hello everyone,

i just wanted to share some problems with you and also the solution.
When trying to build the project with cmake and make i ran into several problems:

1. Problem
mosquitto.c -> The compiler complains about an implicit declaration of function mach_absolute_time().
I solved it with adding the following lines at the top include area:
#if defined(__APPLE__)
     #include <mach/mach_time.h>
#endif

2. Problem
logging.c -> The compiler complains about an implicit declaration of function gettimeofday(&tv, NULL).
I solved it with adding the following lines at the top include area:
#if defined(__APPLE__)
    #include <sys/time.h>
#endif

3.Problem
While trying to link it outputs: "ld: cannot link directly with dylib/framework, your binary is not an allowed client of /usr/lib/libcrypto.dylib for architecture x86_64"
This is because of a restriction in MacOSX starting with Catalina.
I solved this issue with adding the following lines to the CMakeLists.txt at the top:
#MACOSX specific
if (APPLE)
	set(OPENSSL_ROOT_DIR "/usr/local/Cellar/openssl\@1.1/1.1.1h/")
	set(OPENSSL_LIBRARIES "/usr/local/Cellar/openssl\@1.1/1.1.1h/lib/")
	set(OPENSSL_CRYPTO_LIBRARY "/usr/local/Cellar/openssl\@1.1/1.1.1h/lib/libcrypto.dylib")
	set(OPENSSL_SSL_LIBRARY "/usr/local/Cellar/openssl\@1.1/1.1.1h/lib/libssl.dylib")
endif (APPLE)

It should be clear, that the path to openssl has to be adopted regarding to the own needs. 
Another solution would be to handover the values by calling cmake with -D.

Thanks for the nice project and keep up the good work!
Andreas
resolution: Thank you for the tip about openssl.  This helped me, I just had to bump the version number.

title: Details of persistence implementation
body:
Dear community,

I'd like to ask about the implementation of persistence mechanisms in mosquitto: in our use case we really care about persistence of QoS1 messages, i.e. there is a hard requirement not to loose any of the messages once once a PubAck has been sent.

Currently we test with the following config:
autosave_interval 1
autosave_on_changes true
persistence true

The broker behaves as expected during unpredicted shutdowns etc, however I'd like to ask for corner-cases. Can you tell me, whether the PubAck is really sent out once the messages has been saved on the drive? When exactly, e.g. after flushing the file buffers? Maybe you can also tell me the location in the code?
resolution:
The persistence implementation isn't particularly optimised for this very frequent saving of data. Persistence is something I'm planning on looking at in the version after next.

The way it operates at the moment is that for each iteration of the network loop the autosave changes count is checked, and if it exceeds the autosave_interval option, then messages are persisted to disk. This means that when a publish is received there isn't a strict relationship it being persisted to disk and the puback being sent. There is however a pretty limited amount of time between those two events.
